tool.name: fuzzpro
tool.summary: Search protein sequences for deterministic exact or wildcard motifs

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `fuzzpro` searches protein sequence records for one linear motif and reports all matches in stable input order. The Epithema v1 surface keeps the pattern model intentionally narrow: exact amino-acid symbols plus `X` as a one-residue wildcard, no gaps, no indels, and no full historical EMBOSS fuzzy-expression language.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local protein sequence input path and one motif string. Inputs are loaded through the shared Epithema readers for FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank. Records classified as nucleotide are rejected.

sections.title: Pattern Model
sections.content: Supported motif syntax is exact amino-acid text plus `X` as a single-residue wildcard. Pattern and subject sequences are normalized case-insensitively. Overlapping matches are reported. Subject residues are interpreted literally in v1; there is no protein ambiguity-class expansion beyond the wildcard.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with one row per hit. Columns are `record`, `pattern`, `start`, `end`, and `matched`. Coordinates are user-facing 1-based inclusive.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema fuzzpro`. Validation currently covers exact and wildcard matching, overlapping matches, no-hit behavior, invalid-pattern failure, and nucleotide-input rejection.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not implement character classes, mismatches, gaps, or the broader historical EMBOSS fuzzy-pattern language. Empty patterns are rejected during parsing.

artifacts.label: Protein pattern fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture used for deterministic protein motif validation.

examples.title: Search a protein fixture with a wildcard motif
examples.description: Demonstrates deterministic protein motif searching with the `X` one-residue wildcard and stable hit ordering.

expected_outputs.label: Protein motif hits
expected_outputs.description: A stable table report containing one row per protein motif hit with 1-based inclusive coordinates.
